itemListElement.name: Land Rover Range Rover P38A (LH) 2.5 TDS

mainEntity.name: How much does an M51 2.5 TDS engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M51 2.5 TDS typically costs £3,000–£5,500 supply only, with used units from £1,500–£3,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M51 2.5 TDS a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M51 is considered one of BMW's most robust six-cylinder diesels (4.0★), with many examples exceeding 200,000–250,000 miles when properly maintained. Its mechanical injection system and iron-block construction make it exceptionally durable.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M51 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system neglect is the most common cause of failure — the cooling system is vulnerable to corrosion and leaks, and overheating can cause head gasket failure. Injector pump wear and glow plug issues are also common on higher-mileage examples.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the M51?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Land Rover application, the M51 2.5 TDS powered the Range Rover P38A from 1994 to 2002. The engine was also used across BMW's 3, 5, and 7 Series, and the E34 525tds.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover M51 the same as BMW's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M51D25 engine is mechanically identical across BMW and Land Rover fitments. Land Rover P38A applications used a slightly detuned version (136 hp) compared to some BMW applications, but the core engine, block, and internals are the same.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M51 2.5 TDS typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [INDUSTRY ESTIMATE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across BMW and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access and subframe design.

items.question: How much does an M51 2.5 TDS engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned M51 2.5 TDS typically costs £3,000–£5,500 supply only, with used units from £1,500–£3,000. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,500 depending on labour rates and vehicle access.

items.question: Is the BMW M51 2.5 TDS a reliable engine?
items.answer: Yes — the M51 is considered one of BMW's most robust six-cylinder diesels (4.0★), with many examples exceeding 200,000–250,000 miles when properly maintained. Its mechanical injection system and iron-block construction make it exceptionally durable.

items.question: What is the most common M51 failure?
items.answer: Cooling system neglect is the most common cause of failure — the cooling system is vulnerable to corrosion and leaks, and overheating can cause head gasket failure. Injector pump wear (particularly on high-mileage examples) and glow plug issues are also common.

items.question: Which Land Rover models use the M51?
items.answer: In Land Rover application, the M51 2.5 TDS powered the Range Rover P38A from 1994 to 2002. The engine was also used across BMW's 3, 5, and 7 Series, and the E34 525tds.

items.question: Is the Land Rover M51 the same as BMW's version?
items.answer: Yes — the M51D25 engine is mechanically identical across BMW and Land Rover fitments. Land Rover P38A applications used a slightly detuned version (136 hp) compared to some BMW applications, but the core engine, block, and internals are the same.
